name: W3 Push Morning Flow
description: 12 min shoulder/chest opener with more intensity than W1-W2. Warrior flow for dynamic shoulder opening, plank-to-down-dog for active mobility, hollow body hold for core. Same targets, new movements.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Arms overhead, sink into the lunge. Opens hip flexors AND shoulders simultaneously.
30s each side. Open the hips, arms wide. Feel the chest stretch as arms extend.
30s each side. Reach long, side body stretch. Gets into the lats and obliques — great pre-push day.
Smooth transitions. Plank loads the chest/shoulders, down dog stretches them. Best of both worlds.
Lie face down, reach one leg across to the opposite side. Opens chest, shoulders, and hip flexors in one move.
From a hang or doorframe. Pull shoulder blades down and back. Activates stabilizers before you push heavy.
Lower back pressed into floor, arms and legs hovering. This is the gymnastic core position — harder than a plank.
20s each side. Obliques and shoulder stability. Stack your feet or stagger if needed.

tips: 12 min — warrior flow + active mobility + core. More movement than W1-W2 flows.
Warrior flow: think yoga class energy. Breathe into each position.
Scorpion stretch is sneaky good — you'll feel it in your chest and hip flexors
Hollow body hold: if 20s is easy, extend to 30s. If it's impossible, bend your knees.
